title: Vanessa Johnson
content:
People.ContactEmail vjohnson@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2015
- University of British Columbia
Clients appreciate Vanessa’s in-depth understanding of their business and ability to provide expert and tailor-made advice on payments activities and Canadian Financial Regulation.
Vanessa Johnson is a counsel in McCarthy Tétrault’s Banking and Financial Services group, based in the firm’s Vancouver office. She advises clients on sophisticated and complex transactions in banking and financial services, specializing in the payments industry and compliance with related legal and regulatory requirements.
Vanessa understands that participating in the payments ecosystem can be complicated. To this end, she endeavours to keep clients apprised of proposed changes to legislation and regulations impacting the payments industry, including those applicable to fintechs and financial institutions. She works with clients to devise strategies that help them manage legal risk while embracing opportunities to achieve their business goals.
In addition, Vanessa works with clients to strategically draft and negotiate customer, vendor and other agreements relating to payments and banking services, including card programs. She also advises and supports clients with meeting the requirements of Canadian financial regulations, including the Bank Act (Canada), the Trust and Loan Companies Act (Canada), the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act (Canada), the Retail Payment Activities Act (Canada), the Payments Canada bylaws and rules, as well as provincial consumer protection and credit union legislation. She has experience negotiating and communicating with a variety of commercial parties, financial institutions, fintechs, and regulatory and governmental authorities.
Vanessa is a former in-house counsel for a large credit union and was also an associate for a number of years at a large national law firm. These experiences helped her develop expertise advising on public and private mergers and acquisitions, financing transactions, corporate governance, public disclosure, and drafting and negotiating complex commercial agreements.
Vanessa enjoys mentoring junior lawyers and articling students. She was called to the British Columbia bar in 2015, and received her J.D. from the University of British Columbia in 2014 where she completed the Business Law Concentration. She is also licensed to practice in Ontario and is a Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist with ACAMS.
